REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (582 40th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Mar-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch via Court Call for (Successor Trustee)Debra Dolch who appeared. Katie Tostanoski & initial bidder Bill Qi are also present.

The sale was confirmed at $1,162,500.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed on 2/24/2020. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $210,500.00 is required and was presented to the court for filing. Ms. Rasch asked if they could pay fees that were ordered payable in January out of Escrow which would lessen the amount of the bond and the Court indicated that it wanted to stick with the amount that was in the declaration.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).

The Court stated that it received the supplement to the second account and report, but it didn't quite understand the information on the liabilities. Ms. Rasch provided an enlarged copy of the liabilities, stated that $40,200.00 was the initial amount received on the line of credit, but that it was not the ongoing liability and that the amount received is different because of accrued interest. Ms. Rasch also stated that on the second reverse mortgage they got a lump sum amount of $234,804.00, that $422,780.00 was received from the initial reverse mortgage, that the actual amount of the liability includes all of the refinancing costs, closing costs, origination fees, broker's fees, accrued interest and that the total amount owed includes all of those other costs. The Second Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Trausch. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-09-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Authority To Refinance Reverse Mortgage
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Trustee).

Ms. Rasch stated that this is a refinance of an existing first reverse mortgage that has been fully exhausted, that they are obtaining a jumbo loan, that the Lender reduced the amount of the loan from $774,000.00 to $746,000.00 and that all of the funds are paid out in full on the date escrow closes. Ms. Rasch also stated that there is no initiation fee and no monthly service charges, that after the first year the amount owed will be $800,000.00 and that it will keep the Conservatee in her house for about another ten months. In addition, Ms. Rasch stated that the property is worth about $1,300,000.00, that about $500,000.00 will be left in equity, that remaining in the home is the Conservatee's wish and that they have a responsibility to maintain the Conservatee in the most less restrictive environment. The Petition for Authority to Refinance Reverse Mortgage is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Court Supervision And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee

Ex Parte Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Court Supervsion And For Appointment Of Temporary Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Cases PCN-16-299639 and PTR-16-299994 were heard together.
Appearances: Nancy Rasch, for and with petitioner Debra Dolch; Marta Eisenberg, court-appointed attorney for conservatee Katie Hyde; Cornelius ("Connie") Hyde

Ms. Eisenberg stated that Ms. Hyde is adamantly oposed to any conservatorship, but that if one were to be appointed her preference for a family member to serve in that capacity. Additionally, Ms. Hyde objects to the granting of the requested medical powers. Ms. Eisenberg stated that Katie had spoken with her brother Connie as well as with her nieces in England, and was willing to move on a trial basis either to Ireland and live with her brother or England and live with her niece Norma White. However, she does not want any decisions made about her home until after the trial period. Ms. Eisenberg stated that based on information she was given by Connie, she believes that Katie would qualify for free medical care if she were to move to Ireland, but that she has not confirmed this to be true. She added that Katie wants to remain in her home until after her niece Norma White visits her in September. Ms. Eisenberg recommended maintaining the status quo for several months to permit the visit from the neice and trial period for Katie living abroad in either Ireland or England. Cornelius Hyde stated that fromn speaking with Katie, he was aware that she didn't like having caregivers in her home, and that she objected to home care. He stated that he had spoken with someone at the Irish Embassy, who had confirmed that Katie would be eligible for free health care in Ireland if she were living there. Nancy Rasch stated that temporary conservator Debra Dolch was in agreement with the proposal made by Ms. Eisenberg. She added that she would be okay either with a continuance of the temporary conservatorship, or the granting of the permanent conservatorship which could be terminated if Katie decided to remain living abroad. With Connie planning to return to Ireland shortly, an opportunity would arise for the provision of home care services for a six-week period, to see how well that would work for Katie.
The Court ordered that the temporary conservatorship be extended to September 29, 2016, with the hearing on the petition for permanent conservatorship continued to September 29, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 204. Upon the request of Ms. Rasch, the court ordered that the extension order include a provision for an increase in the monthly allowance to Katie Hyde to $1200 per month, and further ordered an authorization to reimburse Cornelius Hyde for travel expenses not to exceed $4500.Ms. Rasch agreed to prepare and submit an appropriate order.
Regarding the petition to place the trust under court supervision, Ms. Eisenberg stated that she favored the granting of that petition. The Court granted the petition to put the trust under court supervision (order signed).
Ms. Eisenberg will remain on as court-appointed attorney for conservatee Katie Hyde.
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Investigator: Cynthia Jones. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)
